reforms.description: The Belgian authorities are raising the quotas of new medical graduates by 60% over 2011-15 to address potential shortages in the healthcare sector, aiming to make the general practitioner profession more attractive.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of medical graduates will help alleviate shortages in healthcare, thereby improving access to medical services and enhancing overall productivity in the health sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities are actively raising quotas as part of their strategy to address healthcare shortages.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to the healthcare workforce strategy, aimed at addressing pressing shortages and improving service delivery.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are raising the quotas of new medical graduates by 60% over 2011-15.
